name: Kadhi Pakora
tagline: Yogurt-besan curry with gram-flour fritters — sour, golden, calming
highlight: Long simmer takes the raw bite off the gram flour and turns yogurt silky

hero.alt: Golden yogurt-besan curry with floating onion pakoras and a red-chili tempering
hero.caption: kadhi pakora — yogurt curry with onion fritters

healthFacts: Yogurt provides calcium, B2, B12, and gut-friendly probiotic strains.
Slow simmer denatures yogurt protein gently — no curdling, full digestibility.
Besan (gram flour) adds plant protein and slow-release carbs.
Fenugreek and curry leaves add iron and subtle blood-sugar regulation.

steps.title: Make the kadhi base
steps.detail: Whisk yogurt, 3 tbsp besan, turmeric, salt, and 4 cups water until smooth — no lumps. Pour into a heavy pot and bring to a gentle boil, stirring constantly to prevent splitting.
steps.tip: Constant stirring for the first 5 minutes is non-negotiable — that's when yogurt curdles.

steps.title: Fry the pakoras
steps.detail: Mix onion, green chili, 2 tbsp besan, salt, and a splash of water into a thick batter. Heat oil to 180°C and drop spoonfuls of batter to fry till golden (3–4 min per batch). Drain on paper.
steps.tip: Pakora batter should be thick — runny batter spreads and absorbs oil.

steps.title: Tadka and finish
steps.detail: Simmer the kadhi on low for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Heat mustard oil in a small pan, crackle mustard seeds, add curry leaves and ginger-garlic paste. Pour the tadka over the kadhi, drop in the pakoras, and let them soak for 5 minutes before serving.
steps.tip: The longer the simmer, the silkier the kadhi — never rush below 20 minutes.
